textByHashes.96bb84c386ed6fbff57dd925bc7ff7b0bbad507a: Well you have helped me in the past. That said, I can't just go running off to Xeric's Lookout for no reason.
textByHashes.748ee66256bf37a897f8b7bcb3837614c016f1fa: Ah, I see. Then the day has finally come.
textByHashes.bf565bae14899ed850e202fad2f4295d2af754d7: There has been discontent with the Council since the day they took control of the kingdom. That was always going to come to a head at some point.
textByHashes.cdfe7373e3cf6d100192b1f40608656d6abb93fe: It seems that point has now arrived. I will depart for Xeric's Lookout shortly.
textByHashes.eada68237ed3ed8719c3923d7fd1a6ca700d79e1: I will. But there are a few things I need to prepare first.
textByHashes.45c2fc9f9d3a15a9549b8ffbc83a97fd48194b91: This is quite the group you've gathered here.
textByHashes.3555e6d9babdd6b90c2fdb5998d7420827c60674: So what do we do with this proof?
textByHashes.15af50a7b14c0e891c181e71f7f193b02847984a: I believe I may have an answer to that.
textByHashes.74c168029df5e4d2323544276a6613abaaf74911: Indeed. This here is the answer to our issue.
textByHashes.0b8ad17565318bf4c4628cc553bb9623317a7375: Once, in a time of great need, the five houses of Kourend took control of this kingdom. Now it falls to us to do so again. It's time for a new accord.
textByHashes.18cd70f3affb2e117a56e802940de98e90bbe87b: In a way. Together we would all be responsible for Kourend, but a single individual would rule over the kingdom.
textByHashes.e95b801d7c0457abc8056707111211bdf4053f0d: Hello there. I'm guessing you want to discuss the Royal Accord?
textByHashes.3767f3291941b3d687a28880f0c7aa7eb07346e7: It's a messy business, but the Council has to go. That means a replacement is needed. A new Royal Accord is our best hope for a peaceful transition of power.
textByHashes.fb453c01d5ff6b3793e0fd8d0b56176ea4cba987: Now, that said, there are some preparations I need to make before we finalise things. Perhaps you could help me with that.
textByHashes.ba3912ee59a1c196ff05d4d0d7c5022e20f5a81b: As with the Royal Accord of Twill, our new accord will see power transferred to the five houses. However, only one of them will rule.
textByHashes.641d5e2d269b05b8572819d511f873724f044b0f: The Royal Accord of Twill saw each of the houses take it in turns to do this, but we may want to take a different approach this time.
textByHashes.b366035892dd500f1569aaf14ccd369790ae122f: I'm almost certain that Lord Arceuus and Lady Piscarilius will not wish to rule. Lady Lovakengj likely won't either. That leaves myself and Lord Shayzien.
textByHashes.2e13eb133bed98dd14cdef30f87192b903315080: Well, that's where my preparations come in. While I can't say I'm too keen on ruling Kourend, I'm not sure if Lord Shayzien is the right man for the job either.
textByHashes.aa7f7493a7b6d503482f58b862c6411537ba421d: Take a look through the pages of Kourend's history and you'll soon see that soldiers do not make for good rulers.
textByHashes.7b1251ae6b49b00d8bcb3f4af769a8b5c3fff759: As such, before any final decision is made, there's something I'd like to look into when it comes to Lord Shayzien.
textByHashes.5be32f527b8e797105a07fa5d3eec1f68b98bbc2: As you probably won't be too surprised to hear, I have people who keep a close eye on things around Kourend. They let me know of any interesting occurrences that could impact Hosidius.
textByHashes.ab184e97edb8c662dee36f4df3e2bd2ee6090c4f: Not too long ago, I was informed that Lord Shayzien went for a private walk in the Kourend Woodland. While there, he stopped off at a small Barbarian Camp.
textByHashes.e2c694dc8f0866d3b2956682430bee1acd6c807b: It is. I'm sure it's nothing to worry about, but I'd like to be sure before we make any big decisions.
textByHashes.e07baae6552a821b79811bd1e9f9d4cd93b7f70c: That would be appreciated. It's directly south of here in the Kourend Woodland.
textByHashes.c091c1f9e9d06f0bba0fb9e29141f6ff5ea3ec43: Hello there. Any luck with that barbarian camp?
textByHashes.335dda688b9b71548b24a5e63b5a77a6d1aa12a2: Very well. Let me know if you discover anything. The camp is directly south of here in the Kourend Woodland.
textByHashes.8a4ae8c76534e253458d86afa230c9a8dd044fd9: So, it's as I feared. Still, that doesn't explain why Lord Shayzien would pay for one of his own citizens to be captured.
textByHashes.5393264875eebd43b61b0008ed232174192d784e: Ah, the discovery at Quidamortem. This is all starting to make sense. It's long been believed that King Shayzien VII eventually became Xeric. That discovery did little to suggest otherwise.
textByHashes.0791b178cd70526059a41083960f6fc86c726dc1: Lord Shayzien wouldn't have been too happy of further evidence that Xeric was part of his family. He must have made arrangements to hide as much of that evidence as possible.
textByHashes.b11bfef019ca998fc71cffd0661d96d102edb69d: Thank you, friend. I think we have all the information we need. I'll have some of my people head to this camp. They'll ensure Phileas is safe.
textByHashes.b106ec062575bb2a93574ed34e14e27e3b6fa818: It's probably best he disappear for a while, for his own safety. I'll make the arrangements.
textByHashes.50c458e40f21ad5e71bea5af139f7553070a85c7: For now, nothing. We should keep this information to ourselves, until the time is right. As for the Royal Accord, I am ready to finalise things when you are.
textByHashes.ac7c10b7bfcc734586411e233afac5ef8e2ed8ed: Hello there. Thank you for your help earlier. You'll be pleased to know that Phileas is now safe.
textByHashes.a7ae2c1062ce3effe94ee9cb704df254706ae7f6: What of the Councillor? Is he not joining us?
textByHashes.a3f8a029fc1abcde00f4d6503cecaa7ab4eca217: Between the five of us, we can remove the Council from power with little violence. But what then? Are we willing to support a new Royal Accord?
textByHashes.6de81c1f152af45d00e0b7530a0f10b644e53fee: It could be a risky move.
textByHashes.2aa3dc8c3422775625c7324f84f127578766d69e: Thank you, Lord Shayzien. Taking the throne is not a duty to be considered lightly, but I believe my family and I have what it takes to shoulder this responsibility.
textByHashes.6211d9775adc629aff64f4e5f538fe388951ab00: My friends, thank you for putting your trust in me. This kingdom has suffered through much of late, but now it is time for that suffering to end.
textByHashes.a28cf04fa9d00779019ca33e2f71b0f962ee0270: In honour of her sacrifice, I name this declaration Rose's Accord. With it, the Kingdom of Great Kourend will no longer be a kingdom divided.
textByHashes.a65cf58218ca3c3e03a61f48ee5bedda45aa12e1: I promise not to let you down.
textByHashes.4672ae8f218a94215eb9ddc2c353d0fdb80eb214: Hello there. No doubt you have questions about what just occurred.
textByHashes.430294d21a212efceb8aa6fdc95ce5c1131ec56e: I'm sorry for keeping you in the dark, my friend. It wasn't anything against you. I just couldn't afford to take any risks.
textByHashes.09be77063cdbb8ee100a4c3a20b3f078f4e8dedf: It was inevitable that one day the Council would fall. Their rule has always been fragile.
textByHashes.ec64973d95f99249f264a17e76c52897f2749187: It gives me no pleasure, but this was needed for the safety of all of Kourend. Apart from Lord Shayzien, the other rulers had no interest in the throne. It was always going to be him or me.
textByHashes.3d48f6f8ef25351d7c87e9f35a8d1526ee5cd16c: It was for the safety of Kourend. Lord Shayzien has a good military mind, but he is no ruler. After all, you saw what he did with Phileas.
textByHashes.c6d0db3e485e57bc67976f2d1c7d952b5cc309a2: To Lord Shayzien, family name is everything. Above all else, he craves glory for himself and his house. Under him, war would have been inevitable.
textByHashes.4500246d9d2bbfb7d507096f74379dd761463786: Varlamore, the kingdom to the south. Our two kingdoms were in conflict less than a hundred years ago, and relations remain sour. It would take very little for that conflict to be reignited.
textByHashes.8a0713c04d15f96c8084fb815158254f48ed4576: I can't say for sure, but it was a risk I did not intend to take.
textByHashes.430c2f51b227c40d4686ab9df334ca9350e1b024: A while ago, I managed to acquire the old journal of King Shayzien VII. I knew that I could use it to discredit Lord Shayzien and his family, so I made some arrangements.
textByHashes.31ed5fa75fae9cbd6a5caff9e91ed82b9f2ddd8f: Apologies for the misdirection. It was indeed me who sent that parcel.
textByHashes.30194d4e19ed2bb6e4956e73f869922bc2222b29: Had I revealed the contents of the journal myself, I would likely have just been dismissed as trying to create a rift between our two houses. Phileas was the perfect third party.
textByHashes.6f84e1f3e96f7e330b772b613fd3a3997c427759: Phileas' disappearance was an interesting twist. I hadn't expected something like that. Still, it actually made things even easier in the end. Once Phileas was safe, I told Lord Shayzien and gave him a choice.
textByHashes.ff9b0c1e600dc72554d626f6a76a03aafd3c5eae: Exactly. The truth about Phileas would have lost him the support of his people and ruined his reputation.
textByHashes.4ab3ce448bcf17cd67715a26ef0b084f28cea223: I didn't want to risk losing the real journal. The one I sent to Phileas was a copy, with many bits removed. While I covered my tracks well, Lord Shayzien must have worked out I was involved.
textByHashes.d37e048b1b4f5ce97067abc4e1756a28295f2b02: Yes. Unfortunately for him, the one you stole was a fake. The real one is somewhere much safer.
textByHashes.1811d692f3ade518f51a49ecd3f11e116a2d2c3e: I can't take too much credit from Artur there. He did do most of the work himself. I'd been looking into the Accord for a while when Artur found my research.
textByHashes.3dfd4b4f05ca0085270a3b070c037653814aa556: When he went missing, I feared the worst. It was a nice surprise when you not only brought him back, but the Accord as well.
textByHashes.da707c8ad7cc16e47ab5bcfd031dca4ec09fb46e: Nor do I like it myself. However, when it comes to politics, there are no perfect options. Instead, all we can do is pick the best option we have.
textByHashes.bac03ad1c327b429e9fb98a1d7ac82307fd6b073: Anyway, we will have plenty of time to discuss this later. For now, the Council needs to be dealt with. You should speak with Commander Fullore.
textByHashes.4f48672b49dd646310443eb98a8cc81a6a4c6219: We will have to talk more later my friend. For now, the Council needs to be dealt with. You should speak with Commander Fullore.
textByHashes.f0a65251952b2ecdcff989623ee451c5f0517770: Councillor Andrews, Councillor Unkar. We are here to present a joint declaration from the five houses of Great Kourend.
textByHashes.a29f026762a467a89a9e3d98183e0792729b6d3e: We, the leaders of the five houses of Great Kourend, declare that the Kourend Council is no longer fit to rule. As such, you are relieved of your positions with immediate effect.
textByHashes.3fe2dc35ca734fc6e93d74982252ed1fd68351ea: Furthermore, you will both hand yourselves over to Lord Shayzien's soldiers. They will take you into custody where you will await your trial.
textByHashes.fe5dcfc6d2664db446424f3a0482b8940b433bfa: I am Lord Kandur Hosidius, ruler of this city. Who are you?
textByHashes.09b3ae656090d395796091c36c4c8ea72b6fc513: Pleased to meet you . It is a shame we could not meet in better circumstances.
textByHashes.2b66a5079fd97f01242f32e6dc9855c2f7059500: Our family is in dire need of assistance. Alas, I do not feel I can trust you enough to involve you in this.
textByHashes.db899e59564d0600cf36b0a31b253ae5588683dd: Our family is in dire need of assistance. My eldest son, Artur, has gone missing and we could desperately use some help in finding him.
textByHashes.c3a5ba6d967a13fd5cdf54b459f64d0a75d61080: Would you be willing to assist us? You will be rewarded if you bring him home safe.
textByHashes.ff055c4e5486f71557591b27e8a04cdc57adf136: Thank you friend. I suggest you begin your search by talking to my family and servants. One of them might know where Artur has gone.
textByHashes.33d78fabe15eef90309e7561f6e497d69d1dfcaf: I'm sorry to hear that. If you change your mind please return to me as soon as you can.
textByHashes.acd109a2b64efb6fc0080ddb104d6ce9a8d4e5c4: Hello there. Have you found my son yet?
textByHashes.849c84eb088c3044bcd29fcf4926e3c7ab67a563: I suggest you begin your search by talking to my family and servants. One of them might know where he's gone.
textByHashes.19af9dcfef573f636f14959077052c7316fde6d8: Hello there. Artur is safely back home again. I hear we have you to thank for that.
textByHashes.4b211655fe628b5adf0f2a26a18de8a4909db395: He also said that you managed to recover the original copy of the Royal Accord of Twill.
textByHashes.783a9a0c590365981b372065e6df47fa9eacf255: Well go fetch it and bring it here.
textByHashes.0044b5d154e25fd44429d5c849852c906f75e86a: Yes. I'll make sure it's well looked after. Now as for that reward I promised.
textByHashes.95a446e9e46949e3e3643bcf83638312633b7ad5: Is there anything else I can do for you?
textByHashes.26685f47a0ecfc6a620c783d36234d4a65dd7d68: I assume you're after that reward I promised you. Here you go.
textByHashes.d0b9781623882eb5d34a8d5ab8bd999705b9082e: I am Kandur Hosidius and I am the lord of Hosidius, the city of farmers and chefs. I live here with my wife, Elena, and my two sons, Artur and Pandur.
textByHashes.e4dd9c6e02ca1c12150785f3a15be82c3ea75792: My family and I have a peaceful existence here. That's mainly because I care not for the politics of the other houses. I instead devote my time to more meaningful pursuits.
textByHashes.965c2f8b89c6fdebb7475a8abe3d0ea659f5c165: Ensuring that Kourend is well fed. We may live in gentle times now but who knows how long it will be that way. Sooner or later another war or long winter will hit us. History has proven that.
textByHashes.8765435e17cb859bda96860dd878a2fdcf387dc2: Anything else I can help you with?
textByHashes.1e26d49a8e0101e359e41d4113462d9073a70e5f: Hosidius is home to the farmers and chefs of Kourend. We are the main food supplier to the kingdom and we ensure that her people are fed.
textByHashes.f505895e3f489d0fdd52382f8c2295caf2078ad3: We also own more land than the other Kourend houses. We need the extra space for all of our crops.
textByHashes.d66dfc1df8d296ee4616b7c596c5ee0db985e8f0: Sometimes. They know it is necessary though. There was once a time when the land was shared equally. But then the great famine came, and Kourend did not have the food to last through it.
textByHashes.ee2d296fff161f70a5313347af18b2dec3d359f7: After that, more space was given to Hosidius. We've had the most land ever since.
textByHashes.e8a2494361ab0412acd2a1120073003d1cf1982d: Plans? My only plan is to keep providing food to this kingdom.
textByHashes.33c692e88ffc8d338df463a4b497e7a2fd085dfa: I did. The Accord has the potential to be a powerful weapon, for words cut deeper than a blade ever can. However, I have no need to use this weapon, not unless the other houses give me reason to.
